conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: There's a new bakery opening down the street next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Single-query retrieval misses paraphrased matches -- 'what did I decide about X' doesn't always surface a memory phrased very differently.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Simplest fix: always run the rewriter and expand every query into three variants before retrieval.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Always rewriting adds latency to every single query even when the original phrasing already matches fine -- only rewrite when initial retrieval confidence is low.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched to a different brand of coffee and I'm not sure I like it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: It's supposed to rain most of next week according to the forecast.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: QueryRewriter must fail open -- if QUERY_REWRITER_API_KEY isn't set or the call errors, retrieval proceeds with the original query, never blocks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked: rewriter calls add real latency and we don't want to eat that cost on every low-confidence query either.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister's flight got delayed by a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office plants finally got watered after being neglected for weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decided: cache rewritten variants per raw query string for the session, so a repeated query doesn't pay the rewrite cost twice. Unblocks moving forward.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Implemented the low-confidence trigger in MemoryEngine's retrieval prefix.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Added the fail-open path with a try/except around the rewriter call.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: what confidence threshold actually counts as 'low' -- still tuning against real queries.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The dentist appointment I've been putting off is finally scheduled.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: wire the per-session rewrite cache into QueryRewriter itself.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return that package before the return window closes.

ground_truth.current_objective: Add confidence-triggered query rewriting to MemoryEngine's retrieval path.
active_decisions: Only rewrite the query when initial retrieval confidence is low, not on every query.
Cache rewritten variants per raw query string for the session to avoid repeated rewrite cost.
superseded_or_rejected: Always rewriting every query into three variants before retrieval.
constraints: QueryRewriter must fail open -- retrieval proceeds with the original query if the rewriter is unavailable or errors.
active_tasks: Wire the per-session rewrite cache into QueryRewriter.
open_questions: What confidence threshold counts as 'low' for triggering a rewrite?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Wire the per-session rewrite cache into QueryRewriter so repeated queries don't pay the rewrite cost twice.
resolved_blockers: The added latency of rewriting on every low-confidence query.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Only rewrite the query when initial retrieval confidence is low, not on every query.
Cache rewritten variants per raw query string for the session to avoid repeated rewrite cost.
QueryRewriter must fail open -- retrieval proceeds with the original query if the rewriter is unavailable or errors.
must_not_state: Always rewriting every query into three variants before retrieval, as a current or recommended approach
The added latency of rewriting on every low-confidence query, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending that QueryRewriter block retrieval when the rewrite call fails or no API key is set.
must_prioritize: Wire the per-session rewrite cache into QueryRewriter so repeated queries don't pay the rewrite cost twice.
